_README: Maps each nation to ACS table B05006 place-of-birth code(s) and NYC DOHMH cuisine_description value(s). b05006 codes are ESTIMATE codes (suffix E); the bake derives the matching MOE code by swapping E->M. cuisines must be EXACT strings from dataset 43nn-pn8j (the bake validates and warns on any zero-match string). Empty b05006 = no clean ACS country detail (the nation falls in an 'Other' regional bucket); such teams render as data_quality 'limited'. Edit freely; re-run scripts 03-05 after changes.

ARG.notes: No 'Argentine' DOHMH tag; Latin American is the closest bucket. Steakhouse/Chimichurri added since Argentine asado spots often file under those. Co-location to Argentine neighborhoods sharpens the result.

URU.notes: No 'Uruguayan' DOHMH tag; shares the Latin American / parrilla bucket with Argentina.

COL.notes: No 'Colombian' DOHMH tag; Latin American bucket. Co-location to Colombian enclaves (Jackson Heights) does the work.

ECU.notes: No 'Ecuadorian' DOHMH tag; Latin American bucket.

PAR.notes: ACS does not break out Paraguay (folded into 'Other South America'), so no diaspora layer; flagged limited. Tiny NYC community.

VEN.notes: No 'Venezuelan' DOHMH tag; Latin American / arepa spots.

USA.notes: Host and home team. 'Diaspora density' is meaningless for the home country, so no choropleth; we still surface American/sports-bar style spots citywide. Flagged host.

CAN.notes: Co-host. Canadian-born New Yorkers are dispersed and there is no distinct Canadian cuisine in DOHMH, so the restaurant layer is empty.

MEX.notes: Co-host with a large, well-mapped diaspora and a clean cuisine tag.

JAM.notes: Caribbean bucket; co-location to Jamaican enclaves (East Flatbush, Bronx) sharpens it.

ENG.notes: ACS reports England separately (code 010). 'English' is a thin DOHMH tag (~125 spots); English-style pubs may file as bars without food service and be under-represented.

SCO.notes: ACS reports Scotland separately (code 011). No 'Scottish' cuisine tag; English/pub bucket.

WAL.notes: ACS folds Wales into 'United Kingdom excluding England and Scotland' (code 009, also includes Northern Ireland), so the diaspora figure is not Wales-specific; flagged in notes.

ESP.notes: CAUTION: in NYC the DOHMH 'Spanish' tag overwhelmingly means Caribbean/Latino Spanish-speaking food, NOT food from Spain. 'Tapas' is the more Spain-specific tag. Co-location to the (small) Spain-born population helps but this pairing is weak.

NED.notes: No distinct Dutch cuisine tag in DOHMH; restaurant layer limited.

BEL.notes: No distinct Belgian cuisine tag; restaurant layer limited.

ITA.notes: Italian is huge in NYC; co-location to Italian-born enclaves (Bensonhurst, Morris Park) is what makes this specific rather than citywide.

CRO.notes: No 'Croatian' tag; Eastern European bucket, weak.

SUI.notes: No distinct Swiss cuisine tag; restaurant layer limited.

POL.notes: Co-location to Greenpoint sharpens the Polish result.

UKR.notes: No 'Ukrainian' tag; Eastern European bucket (East Village, Brighton Beach). 'Russian' added as a culinary-overlap proxy with a caveat.

GRE.notes: Co-location to Astoria sharpens this strongly.

KOR.notes: ACS does not split North/South Korea; in practice nearly all Korea-born New Yorkers are from South Korea. Co-location to Koreatown / Flushing.

KSA.notes: No 'Saudi' tag; broad Middle Eastern bucket, weak.

QAT.notes: ACS folds Qatar into 'Other Western Asia'; no diaspora layer, flagged limited. Tiny NYC community.

UZB.notes: No 'Uzbek' tag; Bukharian/Central Asian food in Rego Park/Forest Hills often files as Eastern European or Middle Eastern. Co-location helps.

MAR.notes: 'Moroccan' is a thin tag (~149); widened to Mediterranean/Middle Eastern with this caveat.

SEN.notes: DOHMH has no nation-level African tags; 'African' is one bucket. Co-location to Senegalese enclaves (Harlem, Bronx) is essential to make this meaningful.

TUN.notes: ACS folds Tunisia into 'Other Northern Africa'; no diaspora layer, flagged limited.

NGA.notes: African bucket; co-location to Nigerian enclaves (Bronx) sharpens it.

GHA.notes: African bucket; co-location to Ghanaian enclaves (Bronx) sharpens it.

MLI.notes: ACS folds Mali into 'Other Western Africa'; no diaspora layer, flagged limited.

NZL.notes: ACS folds New Zealand into 'Other Australian and New Zealand Subregion'; no diaspora layer, flagged limited.
